nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Torono
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmela, Sal, Salvatore.  French Alphonse, Dominique.
habitational name from the city of Turin (Italian Torino) in northern Italy, or from Torino di Sangro (Chieti).

variants.name: Toro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Rafael, Angel, Raul, Jaime, Julio, Miguel, Manuel, Efrain.
Spanish: habitational name from Toro in Zamora province. Compare   De Toro  .

Italian and Spanish: nickname for a lusty person or for someone who owned a bull, or a metonymic occupational name for a tender of bulls or possibly for a bull fighter, from toro ‘bull’ (from Latin taurus). Compare Del Toro .

nameMeanings.name: Fernando
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Fernán
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

en.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono was born in 1107 as the son of Juan de Torono and Toda de Torono. He had at least 1 son with Toda de Torono.

fr.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono est né en 1107, fils de Juan de Torono et Toda de Torono. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Toda de Torono.

de.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono wurde 1107, als Sohn von Juan de Torono und Toda de Torono geboren. Er hatte mit Toda de Torono mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono è nato il nel 1107 come figlio di Juan de Torono e Toda de Torono. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Toda de Torono.

ja.Becerreá, Lugo, Galicia, Espanha: スペインガリシアルゴベセレa
ja.lifeSummary: Fernando de Toronoは1107年にJuan de ToronoとToda de Toronoの息子として生まれた。彼はToda de Toronoとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono(은)는 1107년 Juan de Torono(와)과 Toda de Torono의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Toda de Torono(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono nasceu em 1107 filho de Juan de Torono e Toda de Torono. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Toda de Torono.

ru.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono родился в 1107 г. Его родителей звали Juan de Torono и Toda de Torono. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Toda de Torono.

es.lifeSummary: Fernando de Torono nació en 1107 como hijo de Juan de Torono y Toda de Torono. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Toda de Torono.
